acceptanceSignals: Employees can preorder across multiple future dates in calendar/week views.
Search and filters cover price, type, health tags, deliverability, and exact remaining quantity.
Core discovery remains fully functional with recommendation flags disabled.

attempts.latestEventSummary: feature completed: Employee Discovery and Preorder in 0 attempts

lastCommit.message: feat: implement Employee Discovery and Preorder (attempt 4)

reinforcementResults.name: HTTP Contract Sync Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: The feature’s HTTP behavior changes are contract-synchronized: OpenAPI specs and generated TypeScript artifacts were updated in the same change set, and contract tests cover route-to-spec parity plus error-code/error-schema mapping for the employee discovery and preorder paths.

reinforcementResults.name: No Legacy Path Rule
reinforcementResults.summary: The current workspace satisfies this feature and active policy: employee discovery/preorder supports multi-day week/calendar windows, deterministic search/filter (price, type, health tags, deliverability, exact remaining quantity), consistent Asia/Taipei window/cutoff handling, and backend authoritative remaining-quantity counters, with legacy/fallback paths removed and covered by current-flow tests.

excludedApproaches: Recommendation-first UI that weakens deterministic filtering.
Showing non-deliverable vendors or items to users.
Coarse inventory ranges instead of exact remaining counts.
executionConstraints: Prioritize deterministic search/filter over recommendation behavior for MVP.
Apply Asia/Taipei window/cutoff semantics consistently in UI and API results.
Use backend authoritative inventory counters for remaining quantity display.
name: Employee Discovery and Preorder
rationale: Employee-side usability and transparency are required for adoption at scale.
summary: Deliver multi-day discovery and preorder entry with accurate availability context.
